back_translation: Nahash the Ammonite came up and encamped against Jabesh-gilead, and all the men of Jabesh said to Nahash, 'Cut for us a covenant, and we will serve you.'
Nahash the Ammonite said to them, 'In this I will cut for you — in the gouging out for you of every right eye, and I will set it as a reproach over all Israel.'
The elders of Jabesh said to him, 'Let us alone seven days, and we will send messengers in all the territory of Israel — and if there is no deliverer for us, we will come out to you.'
The messengers came to Gibeah of Saul, and they spoke the words in the ears of the people, and all the people lifted up their voice and wept.
Behold, Saul was coming after the oxen from the field, and Saul said, 'What is to the people, that they weep?' And they told him the words of the men of Jabesh.
The Spirit of God rushed upon Saul at his hearing these words, and his anger burned greatly.

He took a pair of oxen and cut them in pieces, and sent them in all the territory of Israel by the hand of the messengers, saying, 'Whoever does not come out after Saul and after Samuel — so shall be done to his oxen.' And the fear of the LORD fell upon the people, and they came out as one man.
He numbered them at Bezek — and the sons of Israel were three hundred thousand, and the men of Judah thirty thousand.

They said to the messengers who had come, 'Thus you shall say to the men of Jabesh-gilead: Tomorrow there shall be for you deliverance in the heat of the sun.' The messengers came and told the men of Jabesh, and they rejoiced.
The men of Jabesh said, 'Tomorrow we will come out to you, and you shall do to us according to all that is good in your eyes.'

It was on the next day, that Saul set the people as three heads, and they came into the midst of the camp in the morning watch, and they struck the Ammonites until the heat of the day — and it was the survivors were scattered, and there did not remain among them two together.
The people said to Samuel, 'Who is the one who said, Shall Saul reign over us? Give the men, and we will put them to death.'
Saul said, 'No man shall be put to death on this day, for today the LORD has worked deliverance in Israel.'
Samuel said to the people, 'Come, let us go to Gilgal, and let us renew there the kingdom.'

All the people went to Gilgal, and they made Saul king there before the LORD in Gilgal, and they sacrificed there peace sacrifices before the LORD, and Saul and all the men of Israel rejoiced there greatly.
